i. The model should include a maximum acceleration $\alpha$, which represents the strength of the vehicle's acceleration
capability.
ii. The following model needs to consider the influence of speed and following distance on the acceleration of the
following vehicle. The influence of speed and the influence of the following distance should be decoupled, which means
that the factors affecting speed or following distance can be decoupled from the final following model using addition or
subtraction.
iii. The concept of desired following distance should be incorporated into the model. This distance refers to the ideal
gap that a driver wishes to maintain. It is necessary to take into account the smallest following distance $s_{0}$ that
the driver can accept, as well as the time $T$ required for emergency braking in dangerous situations (which can be used
to calculate the braking distance needed at the current driving speed).
iv. The desired speed $v_{0}$ should also be considered in the model. This speed represents the velocity that the driver
aims to achieve.
